methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Critical Energy, a seed-stage company in Climate & Energy founded by Spencer Jackson, is evaluated against a peer universe comprising one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private-market transactions. The valuation analysis relies on a peer-median approach given data availability constraints at the seed stage.

The peer-median method applies to three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A). The median valuation across this set is $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. This method anchors the primary quantitative basis for valuation, reflecting observed pricing in the broader Climate & Energy investment landscape among companies at Series A maturity.

The triangulated valuation range is $128M (low) to $172M (high), centered on $150M median. This narrow 35% spread reflects reliance on a single method with limited input diversity. Key uncertainties include Critical Energy's specific product maturity, capital efficiency relative to peers, and pathway to Series A fundraising—factors not captured in peer medians alone. The analysis would strengthen considerably with additional methodological anchors (revenue multiples, DCF outputs) and granular comparison of technology risk, market timing, and team execution against the named peer set. Current data supports a median valuation of $150M with material uncertainty bounds.
